description_long: This cathedral is a grand, elongated structure built from light tan stone, featuring a prominent central bell tower with crenellated battlements and tall, narrow arched windows. The main nave is lined with a series of pointed Gothic windows, each separated by slender buttresses, and the roofline is adorned with decorative pinnacles. The side aisles and transept arms are slightly lower, with the right wing terminating in a smaller, intricately detailed section topped with balustrades. The main entrance is set within a pointed archway beneath the bell tower, flanked by smaller doors and windows, and the facade is relatively restrained in ornamentation, focusing on verticality and symmetry. The structure is open internally, with visible archways and a clear axial alignment from the main entrance through to the rear. The overall impression is one of vertical elegance and architectural harmony, typical of English Gothic cathedrals.
description: A large, tan stone Gothic cathedral with a tall central bell tower, pointed arched windows, and decorative pinnacles along the roofline. The facade is symmetrical and vertically oriented, with restrained ornamentation and a grand entry portal.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the nave, the tall bell tower on the left, and a series of pointed arched windows and buttresses.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the main entry portal beneath the bell tower, the symmetrical arrangement of windows, and the open interior space.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with the bell tower now on the right and the smaller, balustraded section on the left.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the bell tower, the secondary entryways, and the overall axial alignment of the structure.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: The main facade features pointed arches, crenellations, and decorative pinnacles, with minimal sculptural ornamentation. The design emphasizes vertical lines and symmetry.
attribute_entry_portal_design: The main entry portal is a pointed arch set beneath the bell tower, flanked by smaller doors and windows. It is simply adorned, with the focus on the arch shape and vertical elements rather than elaborate sculpture.
